title: Five workflow patterns from simple to complex

body.overview: Anthropic identifies five workflow patterns of increasing complexity. Each is built from augmented LLMs composed together. Pick the simplest pattern that fits your task.

components.name: Prompt Chaining
components.role: Sequential LLM calls where each processes the previous output. Use when a task decomposes into fixed subtasks (e.g., draft → translate).

components.name: Routing
components.role: Classify input, then direct to a specialized handler. Use when distinct categories need different prompts/tools (e.g., customer service triage).

components.name: Parallelization
components.role: Run LLM calls simultaneously, aggregate results. Two variants: sectioning (split task) and voting (same task, multiple attempts).

components.name: Orchestrator-Workers
components.role: A central LLM dynamically breaks down tasks and delegates to worker LLMs. Use when subtasks can't be predicted in advance.

components.name: Evaluator-Optimizer
components.role: One LLM generates, another evaluates in a loop. Use when there are clear quality criteria and iterative refinement adds value.

body.flow: Assess task → Can it be a single LLM call? Stop. → Fixed subtasks? Prompt chain. → Distinct categories? Route. → Independent subtasks or need confidence? Parallelize. → Unpredictable subtasks? Orchestrator. → Clear quality criteria? Evaluator loop.
